title: lepicephalus transsylvanicus
intro: Lepicephalus transsylvanicus, also known as the Transylvanian bugloss, is a plant species belonging to the family Boraginaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. It is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and promoting wound healing. The plant can be prepared for herbal use through infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, and is often used in folk remedies in Eastern Europe.
